prompt.name: Weather Card
prompt.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
prompt.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

prompt.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
prompt.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
prompt.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

prompt.name: Kanban Task Board
prompt.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
prompt.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

prompt.name: Interactive Periodic Table
prompt.description: Periodic table grid with element details, search/filter, and color coding
prompt.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements. Requirements:
- Grid layout matching the standard periodic table shape (18 columns, with gaps for lanthanides/actinides)
- Each element cell shows: symbol (large), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code cells by element category (alkali metals, noble gases, transition metals, etc.) using design system color tokens
- Clicking an element shows a detail panel with: name, symbol, atomic number, atomic weight, electron configuration, and category
- Search/filter input at the top that filters elements by name or symbol
- Smooth transitions when filtering
- A legend showing category-to-color mapping

All element data must come from props as an array. Use the design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

prompt.name: E-Commerce Product Page
prompt.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
prompt.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

prompt.name: Real-Time Chat Interface
prompt.description: Chat UI with ggui:agent-data listener, message list, and sendSpec
prompt.prompt:
Build a real-time chat interface. The component receives messages via the ggui:agent-data window event.

Layout:
- Chat header showing the conversation title and participant count from props
- Scrollable message list area (auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages)
- Each message bubble shows: sender name, message text, timestamp
- Messages from the current user (user.id from props) align right with primary color
- Messages from others align left with gray background
- Typing indicator when someone is typing (received via ggui:agent-data with type: 'typing')

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp } when send is clicked or Enter is pressed

Real-time data handling:
- Listen for window event 'ggui:agent-data'
- Event detail shape: { type: 'message', sender: string, text: string, timestamp: string }
  OR { type: 'typing', sender: string }
- Append new messages to the list, show/hide typing indicator

Output a __GGUI_SEND_SPEC__ describing the expected data shape.
All initial messages and user info from props. Use design system CSS variables.

initialMessages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups

prompt.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
prompt.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

prompt.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
prompt.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
prompt.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

prompt.name: Kanban Task Board
prompt.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
prompt.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

prompt.name: Interactive Periodic Table
prompt.description: Periodic table grid with element details, search/filter, and color coding
prompt.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements. Requirements:
- Grid layout matching the standard periodic table shape (18 columns, with gaps for lanthanides/actinides)
- Each element cell shows: symbol (large), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code cells by element category (alkali metals, noble gases, transition metals, etc.) using design system color tokens
- Clicking an element shows a detail panel with: name, symbol, atomic number, atomic weight, electron configuration, and category
- Search/filter input at the top that filters elements by name or symbol
- Smooth transitions when filtering
- A legend showing category-to-color mapping

All element data must come from props as an array. Use the design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

function ElementCell({ element, isSelected, isFiltered, onClick, motionEnabled }:
  { element:Element; isSelected:boolean; isFiltered:boolean; onClick:(e:Element)=>void; motionEnabled:boolean }) {
  const c = CAT[element.category] ?? CAT['Unknown'];
  return (
    <div
      onClick={() => onClick(element)}
      role="button" tabIndex={0}
      aria-label={element.name}
      title={element.name}
      onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key==='Enter'||e.key===' ') onClick(element); }}
      style={{
        position:'relative', width:'100%', aspectRatio:'1/1',
        borderRadius:'var(--ggui-shape-radius-sm,4px)',
        border:'1.5px solid', borderColor: isSelected ? SELECTED_BD : c.bd,
        background:c.bg, color:c.tx, cursor:'pointer',
        display:'flex', flexDirection:'column', alignItems:'center', justifyContent:'center',
        padding:'var(--ggui-spacing-1,4px)', boxSizing:'border-box',
        opacity: isFiltered ? 0.12 : 1,
        transform: isSelected ? 'scale(1.08)' : 'scale(1)',
        boxShadow: isSelected
          ? '0 0 0 2px var(--ggui-color-neutral-900,#111827),var(--ggui-shape-shadow-lg,0 10px 15px -3px rgba(0,0,0,.1))'
          : 'var(--ggui-shape-shadow-sm,0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.05))',
        transition: motionEnabled ? 'opacity 250ms ease,transform 150ms ease,box-shadow 150ms ease' : 'none',
        zIndex: isSelected ? 2 : 1, userSelect:'none',
      }}>
      <span style={{ position:'absolute', top:'var(--ggui-spacing-1,4px)', left:'var(--ggui-spacing-1,4px)',
        fontSize:'0.45em', fontWeight:'var(--ggui-font-weight-semibold,600)', lineHeight:1, opacity:0.9 }}>
        {element.atomicNumber}
      </span>
      <span style={{ fontSize:'clamp(7px,1vw,15px)', fontWeight:'var(--ggui-font-weight-bold,700)',
        lineHeight:1, letterSpacing:'-0.02em', marginTop:'var(--ggui-spacing-2,8px)' }}>
        {element.symbol}
      </span>
    </div>
  );
}

function DetailPanel({ element, onClose, motionEnabled }:
  { element:Element; onClose:()=>void; motionEnabled:boolean }) {
  const c = CAT[element.category] ?? CAT['Unknown'];
  const bv = BV_MAP[c.bv] ?? 'default';
  return (
    <Card padding={20} shadow="lg" style={{ animation: motionEnabled ? animation.scaleIn : 'none' }}>
      <Stack direction="vertical" gap={12}>
        <Row align="center" justify="between">
          <Row align="center" gap={12}>
            <div style={{ width:64, height:64, borderRadius:'var(--ggui-shape-radius-md,8px)',
              background:c.bg, border:'2px solid', borderColor:c.bd, color:c.tx,
              display:'flex', flexDirection:'column', alignItems:'center', justifyContent:'center', flexShrink:0 }}>
              <span style={{ fontSize:'var(--ggui-font-size-xs,12px)', opacity:0.85, lineHeight:1 }}>{element.atomicNumber}</span>
              <span style={{ fontSize:'var(--ggui-font-size-2xl,24px)', fontWeight:'var(--ggui-font-weight-bold,700)', lineHeight:1.1 }}>{element.symbol}</span>
            </div>
            <Stack direction="vertical" gap={4}>
              <Text variant="h3" weight="bold">{element.name}</Text>
              <Badge variant={bv} size="sm">{element.category}</Badge>
            </Stack>
          </Row>
          <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" onClick={onClose} aria-label="Close detail">
            <Icon name="x" size={16} />
          </Button>
        </Row>
        <Divider margin={4} />
        <div style={{ display:'grid', gridTemplateColumns:'1fr 1fr', gap:'var(--ggui-spacing-2,8px)' }}>
          {([['Atomic Number',String(element.atomicNumber)],['Atomic Weight',element.atomicWeight],
             ['Symbol',element.symbol],['Category',element.category]] as [string,string][]).map(([label,value]) => (
            <Box key={label} padding={8} background="var(--ggui-color-neutral-50,#f9fafb)" borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-sm,4px)">
              <Stack direction="vertical" gap={2}>
                <Text variant="caption" color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-500,#6b7280)">{label}</Text>
                <Text variant="label" weight="semibold">{value}</Text>
              </Stack>
            </Box>
          ))}
        </div>
        <Box padding={10} background="var(--ggui-color-primary-50,#f0f9ff)" borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-sm,4px)">
          <Stack direction="vertical" gap={4}>
            <Text variant="caption" color="var(--ggui-color-primary-700,#0369a1)">Electron Configuration</Text>
            <Text variant="label" weight="medium" color="var(--ggui-color-primary-900,#0c4a6e)">{element.electronConfiguration}</Text>
          </Stack>
        </Box>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  );
}

function Legend() {
  return (
    <Card padding={12} shadow="none">
      <Stack direction="vertical" gap={8}>
        <Text variant="label" weight="semibold" color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-700,#374151)">Category Legend</Text>
        <div style={{ display:'grid', gridTemplateColumns:'repeat(auto-fill,minmax(140px,1fr))', gap:'var(--ggui-spacing-1,4px)' }}>
          {Object.entries(CAT).map(([cat, c]) => (
            <div key={cat} style={{ display:'flex', alignItems:'center', gap:'var(--ggui-spacing-1,4px)' }}>
              <div style={{ width:14, height:14, borderRadius:'var(--ggui-shape-radius-sm,4px)',
                background:c.bg, border:'1.5px solid', borderColor:c.bd, flexShrink:0 }} />
              <span style={{ fontSize:'var(--ggui-font-size-xs,12px)', color:'var(--ggui-color-neutral-700,#374151)', lineHeight:1.3 }}>{cat}</span>
            </div>
          ))}
        </div>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  );
}

export default function GeneratedComponent({ elements = DEFAULT_ELEMENTS }: Props) {
  const { motionEnabled } = useMotion();
  const [search, setSearch] = useState('');
  const [selected, setSelected] = useState<Element | null>(null);

  const matchingNums = useMemo(() => {
    const q = search.trim().toLowerCase();
    if (!q) return new Set(elements.map(e => e.atomicNumber));
    return new Set(elements.filter(e =>
      e.name.toLowerCase().includes(q) || e.symbol.toLowerCase().includes(q)
    ).map(e => e.atomicNumber));
  }, [search, elements]);

  const posMap = useMemo(() => {
    const m = new Map<string,Element>();
    elements.forEach(el => m.set(el.period + '-' + el.group, el));
    return m;
  }, [elements]);

  const handleClick = (el: Element) =>
    setSelected(prev => prev?.atomicNumber === el.atomicNumber ? null : el);

  const renderCell = (period: number, group: number) => {
    const el = posMap.get(period + '-' + group);
    if (!el) {
      if ((period === 6 || period === 7) && group === 3) {
        const isLa = period === 6;
        return (
          <div key={'ph-' + period + '-' + group} style={{
            width:'100%', aspectRatio:'1/1',
            borderRadius:'var(--ggui-shape-radius-sm,4px)',
            border:'1.5px dashed',
            borderColor: isLa ? 'var(--ggui-color-primary-700,#0369a1)' : 'var(--ggui-color-primary-800,#075985)',
            background: isLa ? 'var(--ggui-color-primary-100,#e0f2fe)' : 'var(--ggui-color-primary-200,#bae6fd)',
            display:'flex', alignItems:'center', justifyContent:'center',
            fontSize:'0.5em', fontWeight:'var(--ggui-font-weight-bold,700)',
            color: isLa ? 'var(--ggui-color-primary-700,#0369a1)' : 'var(--ggui-color-primary-800,#075985)',
          }}>{isLa ? '57–71' : '89–103'}</div>
        );
      }
      return <div key={'e-' + period + '-' + group} />;
    }
    return (
      <div key={el.atomicNumber} style={{ gridColumn:group, gridRow:period }}>
        <ElementCell element={el} isSelected={selected?.atomicNumber===el.atomicNumber}
          isFiltered={!matchingNums.has(el.atomicNumber)} onClick={handleClick} motionEnabled={motionEnabled} />
      </div>
    );
  };

  const cells: React.ReactNode[] = [];
  for (let p=1; p<=9; p++) for (let g=1; g<=18; g++) cells.push(renderCell(p, g));

  const labelStyle: React.CSSProperties = {
    fontSize:'var(--ggui-font-size-xs,12px)',
    color:'var(--ggui-color-neutral-500,#6b7280)',
    fontWeight:'var(--ggui-font-weight-medium,500)',
  };

  return (
    <>
      <MotionKeyframes />
      <Container maxWidth="full" padding={16}>
        <Stack direction="vertical" gap={12}>
          <Row align="center" justify="between" gap={16}>
            <Stack direction="vertical" gap={2}>
              <Text variant="h2" weight="bold" color="var(--ggui-color-primary-800,#075985)">Periodic Table of Elements</Text>
              <Text variant="caption" color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-500,#6b7280)">{elements.length} elements · Click any element for details</Text>
            </Stack>
            <div style={{ minWidth:220 }}>
              <Input label="Search elements" placeholder="Search by name or symbol…" value={search} onChange={setSearch} />
            </div>
          </Row>

          {selected && (
            <div style={{ animation: motionEnabled ? animation.slideInDown : 'none', maxWidth:460 }}>
              <DetailPanel element={selected} onClose={() => setSelected(null)} motionEnabled={motionEnabled} />
            </div>
          )}

          <Card padding={8} shadow="sm">
            <div style={{ display:'grid', gridTemplateColumns:'var(--ggui-spacing-4,16px) repeat(18,minmax(0,1fr))',
              gap:'var(--ggui-spacing-1,4px)', marginBottom:'var(--ggui-spacing-1,4px)' }}>
              <div />
              {Array.from({length:18},(_,i) => (
                <div key={i} style={{ textAlign:'center' }}><span style={labelStyle}>{i+1}</span></div>
              ))}
            </div>
            <div style={{ display:'flex', gap:'var(--ggui-spacing-1,4px)' }}>
              <div style={{ display:'grid', gridTemplateRows:'repeat(9,1fr)', gap:'var(--ggui-spacing-1,4px)',
                width:'var(--ggui-spacing-4,16px)', flexShrink:0 }}>
                {['1','2','3','4','5','6','7','*','**'].map(l => (
                  <div key={l} style={{ display:'flex', alignItems:'center', justifyContent:'center' }}>
                    <span style={labelStyle}>{l}</span>
                  </div>
                ))}
              </div>
              <div style={{ flex:1, display:'grid', gridTemplateColumns:'repeat(18,minmax(0,1fr))',
                gridTemplateRows:'repeat(9,1fr)', gap:'var(--ggui-spacing-1,4px)' }}>
                {cells}
              </div>
            </div>
            <div style={{ marginTop:'var(--ggui-spacing-2,8px)' }}>
              <Text variant="caption" color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-500,#6b7280)">
                * Lanthanides (57–71)  ·  ** Actinides (89–103)
              </Text>
            </div>
          </Card>

          <Legend />
        </Stack>
      </Container>
    </>
  );
}

generation.sourceCode:
import { Card, Stack, Row, Text, Divider, Box } from '@ggui-ai/design/primitives';

interface ForecastDay {
  dayName: string;
  condition: string;
  icon: string;
  highTemp: string;
  lowTemp: string;
}

interface Props {
  location?: string;
  currentTemperature: string;
  weatherCondition: string;
  weatherIcon: string;
  humidity: string;
  windSpeed: string;
  forecast: ForecastDay[];
}

export default function GeneratedComponent({
  location = 'Current Location',
  currentTemperature,
  weatherCondition,
  weatherIcon,
  humidity,
  windSpeed,
  forecast,
}: Props) {
  return (
    <Card
      padding="var(--ggui-spacing-6, 24px)"
      shadow="md"
      border
    >
      <Stack gap={16}>
        <Stack gap={4}>
          <Text
            variant="label"
            color="var(--ggui-color-primary-700, #0369a1)"
          >
            {location}
          </Text>

          <Row justify="between" align="end" gap={8}>
            <Text
              variant="h1"
              color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-900, #111827)"
            >
              {currentTemperature}
            </Text>
            <Stack direction="horizontal" align="center" gap={8}>
              <Text
                variant="h2"
                color="var(--ggui-color-primary-600, #0284c7)"
              >
                {weatherIcon}
              </Text>
              <Text
                variant="body"
                color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-700, #374151)"
                weight="medium"
              >
                {weatherCondition}
              </Text>
            </Stack>
          </Row>
        </Stack>

        <Row gap={12}>
          <Box
            padding="var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)"
            background="var(--ggui-color-primary-50, #f0f9ff)"
            borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-lg, 12px)"
          >
            <Stack gap={4}>
              <Text
                variant="caption"
                color="var(--ggui-color-primary-700, #0369a1)"
              >
                Humidity
              </Text>
              <Text
                variant="h3"
                color="var(--ggui-color-primary-900, #0c4a6e)"
              >
                {humidity}
              </Text>
            </Stack>
          </Box>

          <Box
            padding="var(--ggui-spacing-4, 16px)"
            background="var(--ggui-color-primary-50, #f0f9ff)"
            borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-lg, 12px)"
          >
            <Stack gap={4}>
              <Text
                variant="caption"
                color="var(--ggui-color-primary-700, #0369a1)"
              >
                Wind
              </Text>
              <Text
                variant="h3"
                color="var(--ggui-color-primary-900, #0c4a6e)"
              >
                {windSpeed}
              </Text>
            </Stack>
          </Box>
        </Row>

        <Divider margin={4} />

        <Stack gap={8}>
          <Text
            variant="label"
            color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-500, #6b7280)"
          >
            5-Day Forecast
          </Text>

          <Row gap={8} wrap>
            {forecast.slice(0, 5).map((day) => (
              <Box
                key={day.dayName}
                padding="var(--ggui-spacing-2, 8px)"
                background="var(--ggui-color-neutral-50, #f9fafb)"
                borderRadius="var(--ggui-shape-radius-md, 8px)"
              >
                <Stack align="center" gap={4}>
                  <Text
                    variant="caption"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-700, #374151)"
                  >
                    {day.dayName}
                  </Text>
                  <Text
                    variant="body"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-primary-600, #0284c7)"
                  >
                    {day.icon}
                  </Text>
                  <Text
                    variant="caption"
                    color="var(--ggui-color-neutral-900, #111827)"
                    weight="medium"
                  >
                    {day.highTemp} / {day.lowTemp}
                  </Text>
                </Stack>
              </Box>
            ))}
          </Row>
        </Stack>
      </Stack>
    </Card>
  );
}

prompt.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
prompt.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
prompt.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

prompt.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
prompt.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

prompt.name: Interactive Periodic Table
prompt.description: Periodic table grid with element details, search/filter, and color coding
prompt.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements. Requirements:
- Grid layout matching the standard periodic table shape (18 columns, with gaps for lanthanides/actinides)
- Each element cell shows: symbol (large), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code cells by element category (alkali metals, noble gases, transition metals, etc.) using design system color tokens
- Clicking an element shows a detail panel with: name, symbol, atomic number, atomic weight, electron configuration, and category
- Search/filter input at the top that filters elements by name or symbol
- Smooth transitions when filtering
- A legend showing category-to-color mapping

All element data must come from props as an array. Use the design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

prompt.name: E-Commerce Product Page
prompt.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
prompt.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

prompt.name: Real-Time Chat Interface
prompt.description: Chat UI with ggui:agent-data listener, message list, and sendSpec
prompt.prompt:
Build a real-time chat interface. The component receives messages via the ggui:agent-data window event.

Layout:
- Chat header showing the conversation title and participant count from props
- Scrollable message list area (auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages)
- Each message bubble shows: sender name, message text, timestamp
- Messages from the current user (user.id from props) align right with primary color
- Messages from others align left with gray background
- Typing indicator when someone is typing (received via ggui:agent-data with type: 'typing')

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp } when send is clicked or Enter is pressed

Real-time data handling:
- Listen for window event 'ggui:agent-data'
- Event detail shape: { type: 'message', sender: string, text: string, timestamp: string }
  OR { type: 'typing', sender: string }
- Append new messages to the list, show/hide typing indicator

Output a __GGUI_SEND_SPEC__ describing the expected data shape.
All initial messages and user info from props. Use design system CSS variables.

initialMessages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm

prompt.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
prompt.prompt:

- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

prompt.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
prompt.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
prompt.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

prompt.name: Kanban Task Board
prompt.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
prompt.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

prompt.description: Periodic table grid with element details, search/filter, and color coding
prompt.prompt:

- Grid layout matching the standard periodic table shape (18 columns, with gaps for lanthanides/actinides)
- Each element cell shows: symbol (large), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code cells by element category (alkali metals, noble gases, transition metals, etc.) using design system color tokens
- Clicking an element shows a detail panel with: name, symbol, atomic number, atomic weight, electron configuration, and category
- Search/filter input at the top that filters elements by name or symbol
- Smooth transitions when filtering
- A legend showing category-to-color mapping

All element data must come from props as an array. Use the design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

prompt.name: E-Commerce Product Page
prompt.description: Product detail page with image, tabs, quantity selector, and cart
prompt.prompt:
Build an e-commerce product detail page. Layout:

Top section:
- Product image placeholder (use a colored box with the product name)
- Product title, price (with currency from props), and rating stars (1-5)
- Stock status badge ("In Stock" green or "Low Stock" warning)

Middle section — Tab navigation with 3 tabs:
- "Description" tab: renders product description text from props
- "Specifications" tab: renders key-value pairs from props as a table
- "Reviews" tab: renders a list of reviews (author, rating, text, date) from props

Bottom section:
- Quantity selector (increment/decrement buttons, min 1, max from stock)
- "Add to Cart" button (disabled if out of stock)
- Call props.onAddToCart with { productId, quantity } when clicked

Requirements:
- All product data from props — nothing hardcoded
- Use design system tokens for all colors, spacing, typography
- Price should format with currency symbol from props
- Rating stars should be visual (filled/empty)
- Responsive: stack image above details on mobile widths

prompt.name: Real-Time Chat Interface
prompt.description: Chat UI with ggui:agent-data listener, message list, and sendSpec
prompt.prompt:
Build a real-time chat interface. The component receives messages via the ggui:agent-data window event.

Layout:
- Chat header showing the conversation title and participant count from props
- Scrollable message list area (auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages)
- Each message bubble shows: sender name, message text, timestamp
- Messages from the current user (user.id from props) align right with primary color
- Messages from others align left with gray background
- Typing indicator when someone is typing (received via ggui:agent-data with type: 'typing')

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp } when send is clicked or Enter is pressed

Real-time data handling:
- Listen for window event 'ggui:agent-data'
- Event detail shape: { type: 'message', sender: string, text: string, timestamp: string }
  OR { type: 'typing', sender: string }
- Append new messages to the list, show/hide typing indicator

Output a __GGUI_SEND_SPEC__ describing the expected data shape.
All initial messages and user info from props. Use design system CSS variables.

initialMessages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
